behaviors.description: Reject invalid signature with 401 and do not forward body
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local handler logic (HMAC check + branch) and a single side-effect suppression (`enqueue` not called), not a multi-module state handoff failure. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce mismatch rejection. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI steps. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System were considered, but behavior text is limited to `signature does not match ... rejected with 401; body is not forwarded`, which is fully checkable at handler function boundary with spies.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the handler with a raw body and intentionally wrong `X-Signature-256`; assert status 401 and assert queue-forwarding method was never called.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an ordering contract across modules (HTTP handler ↔ queue writer), so pure unit of one helper is too low. REAL-INFRA: real network/OS is not required; in-process wiring can verify response is blocked until queue write resolves. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered because HTTP is involved, but behavior text only requires ordering (`written ... before ... 200`) and does not require real socket/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler + real queue adapter interface in-process; make queue `write` complete via controllable promise; verify no 200 is emitted before promise resolves, then 200 after resolve.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is about handoff between enqueue path and downstream processor lifecycle, which is a module interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA: no need for real subprocess/network; an unresolved downstream promise reproduces the failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because text specifies waiting behavior, not OS-level process semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue + downstream worker stub where processing never resolves; assert HTTP response is returned after enqueue succeeds and before processing completion.

behaviors.description: Respond within 3 seconds wall-clock under slow concurrent downstream load
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency is sensitive to runtime scheduling/backpressure; fake timers/in-process mocks can hide real latency and starvation failures. Thus real server/runtime timing is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely HTTP latency/concurrency (`within 3 seconds wall-clock`) with no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP service and persistent queue stack; generate concurrent prior events with intentionally slow downstream processing; send a new webhook and assert end-to-end response time stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: Do not write duplicate event ID to queue twice
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness is at state handoff boundary (request handling + persisted seen-id/queue write path), not only a pure helper. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process modules and local persistence test double/DB; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System considered for race risk, but behavior text does not require OS/network phenomena; it states logical dedup (`already received ... not written ... second time`).
behaviors.test_description: Issue two requests with same event ID through wired handler+queue/dedup store; assert first write accepted and second not written (or idempotent no-op), with queue write count remaining one.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and Retry-After header
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundary mapping queue-capacity error to HTTP semantics. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; queue module can signal `full` in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text focuses on translation of queue state to response (`returns 503 with Retry-After`), not real infrastructure timing/network faults.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue implementation configured to report `full`; send webhook and assert status 503 plus correct `Retry-After` header, and no downstream processing trigger.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is queue component behavior across config + persistence/write path; pure unit of one function is too low. REAL-INFRA: level defs place local DB/filesystem-backed component tests in Integration; no subprocess/external API required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System considered due persistence, but behavior text says `persistent queue ... enforces configured capacity` without requiring real network/subprocess/OS primitives beyond local component wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against local test storage with capacity N; enqueue N events successfully, then assert enqueue N+1 is rejected with the queue-full error contract.
